target.title: feat: show in-flight feedback on manual refresh
target.body: ## Problem Clicking **Refresh** while the menu is open gives no immediate feedback. The currently-visible provider card keeps showing its old `Updated …` subtitle, and the persistent **Refresh** row looks idle, until the network round-trip (0.5–5s) completes. The "Refreshing…" state only became visible after switching to a *different* card. Root cause: the open root menu is intentionally **not** rebuilt during NSMenu tracking (the deferred-rebuild policy that avoids mid-tracking layout jumps / freezes). The "Refreshing…" state already existed in the model, but nothing pushed it onto the *already-open* views — so it never rendered until a rebuild happened on the next menu open. Because there's no feedback, users click Refresh repeatedly, and each click fires another cookie-based backend request. ## Fix Surface the in-flight state **in place**, without rebuilding the menu during tracking: - **Provider card subtitle** — a narrowly-scoped `MenuCardRefreshMonitor` bridge over the observable `UsageStore` is injected via a SwiftUI `@Entry` environment value. The header view reads `isRefreshingIndicatorVisible(for:)` and swaps the subtitle to `Refreshing…` in place. Existing `.error` subtitles remain authoritative, including errors derived outside `UsageStore.error`, so the multi-line error layout and copy affordance are never replaced. - **Persistent Refresh row** — the leading `arrow.clockwise` icon is swapped for a spinner occupying the **same fixed 18pt slot** (icon faded via `alphaValue`, not `isHidden`, so the `NSStackView` slot never collapses and the title doesn't shift). The spinner is forced on at click for instant feedback, then synced to `store.isRefreshing` from the store observation (covering both success and failure paths). Both updates are in-place AppKit/SwiftUI mutations on existing views — no `NSMenu` rebuild — so they're safe during menu tracking and don't reintroduce the layout-jump regressions the deferral policy guards against. ## Tests 3 new tests in `StatusMenuPersistentRefreshTests` (7/7 in the suite pass): - spinner toggling keeps fixed row metrics (height/intrinsic/fitting unchanged) - persistent refresh rows reflect store refresh state in place - the monitor mirrors the store's refreshing-indicator gate `swift build` clean; full suite green. Maintainer follow-up on rebased head: - preserve existing error subtitles during live refresh - switch the spinner to a high-contrast appearance while the row is highlighted - add changelog credit - `swift test --filter StatusMenuPersistentRefreshTests` (7/7) - `make check` - Codex autoreview: clean ## Proof
target.author: hhh2210
